questions.question: What was the name of the ship on which the Pilgrims sailed to America in 1620?
questions.explanation: The Mayflower carried 102 passengers and about 30 crew members from Plymouth, England to Plymouth, Massachusetts in 1620. The voyage took 66 days. The Pilgrims signed the Mayflower Compact aboard the ship, which was one of the first governing documents in the New World.
questions.memory_hook: Mayflower — flowers bloomed in a new world.

questions.question: Which country was the first to give women the right to vote in a national election in Europe?
questions.explanation: Finland was the first European country to give women the right to vote in national elections in 1906. Finnish women were also the first in the world to be eligible to stand for election. In 1907, 19 women were elected to the Finnish Parliament.
questions.memory_hook: Finland was first in Europe for female suffrage — a Nordic pioneer.

questions.question: What is the name of the award given to the best player in the NHL each season?
questions.explanation: The Hart Memorial Trophy is awarded annually to the player judged most valuable to his team in the NHL. It has been awarded since 1924. Wayne Gretzky won it a record nine times. The Vezina Trophy goes to the best goaltender, and the Norris to the best defenseman.
questions.memory_hook: Hart Trophy = Heart of the team. MVP of hockey.

questions.question: How many events are in a decathlon?
questions.explanation: A decathlon consists of ten events spread over two days: 100m, long jump, shot put, high jump, and 400m on day one; 110m hurdles, discus, pole vault, javelin, and 1500m on day two. The winner is often called 'the world's greatest athlete.'
questions.memory_hook: Deca = ten. Decathlon = 10 events, one champion.

questions.question: In which city is the famous Wembley Stadium located?
questions.explanation: Wembley Stadium is located in Wembley, London, England. The current stadium opened in 2007 and has a capacity of 90,000, making it the largest stadium in the UK. It hosts FA Cup finals, England national team matches, and major concerts.
questions.memory_hook: Wembley = London. The arch over the stadium is its calling card.

questions.question: Which film series features a character who says 'May the Force be with you'?
questions.explanation: 'May the Force be with you' is the iconic phrase from the Star Wars franchise, first used in the original 1977 film. The Force is a metaphysical power that connects all living things in the Star Wars universe. May 4 is celebrated as 'Star Wars Day' based on a pun on the phrase.
questions.memory_hook: May the Force — May 4 = Star Wars Day. May the fourth be with you.

questions.question: Which artist released the albums 'Lemonade' and 'Renaissance'?
questions.explanation: Beyoncé released 'Lemonade' as a visual album in 2016 and 'Renaissance' in 2022. 'Lemonade' received widespread critical acclaim and was named album of the year by numerous publications. 'Renaissance' debuted at number one and won the Grammy for Best Dance/Electronic Album.
questions.memory_hook: Beyoncé made lemonade when life gave her lemons — and a Renaissance followed.

questions.question: Which director made 'E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial' and 'Jaws'?
questions.explanation: Steven Spielberg directed both Jaws (1975) and E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982). Jaws is credited with creating the 'summer blockbuster' concept. E.T. was the highest-grossing film of all time for 11 years until Jurassic Park (also Spielberg) surpassed it.
questions.memory_hook: Spielberg made the shark scary and the alien lovable. Opposite ends of the spectrum.

questions.question: The 'Trojan War' was fought over which city?
questions.explanation: The Trojan War, as described in Homer's Iliad, was fought between the Greeks and the city of Troy (in present-day Turkey). It began when Paris of Troy took Helen from Sparta. The Greeks used the Trojan Horse to infiltrate the city after a 10-year siege.
questions.memory_hook: Trojan Horse, Trojan War, Trojan everything — Troy was at the center.

questions.question: Which scientist is known for discovering penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928 when he noticed that a mold (Penicillium notatum) was killing bacteria in one of his petri dishes. This accidental discovery led to the development of antibiotics, which have saved an estimated 200 million lives.
questions.memory_hook: Fleming's mold killed bacteria — a dirty lab dish changed medicine forever.

questions.question: Angel Falls, the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall, is located in which country?
questions.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela drops 979 meters (3,212 feet), making it the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall. It cascades off Auyán-tepui, a flat-topped mountain in the Canaima National Park. It was named after American aviator Jimmie Angel, who flew over it in 1933 and brought it to the world's attention.
questions.memory_hook: Angel Falls = Venezuela. Nearly 1 km of freefall. Named after an American pilot who spotted it from his plane. The water atomizes into mist before it reaches the bottom.

questions.question: Which blood type is considered the universal donor for red blood cell transfusions?
questions.explanation: O negative blood lacks the A, B, and Rh antigens, so it can be transfused into patients of any blood type without triggering an immune reaction. It is crucial in emergencies when there is no time to type a patient's blood. Only about 7% of people have O negative blood, making it particularly valuable.
questions.memory_hook: O negative = universal donor. No antigens to trigger rejection. It's the emergency room blood type — given when there is no time to check compatibility.
